treatment_information.specific_treatment: removal of ACTH-producing metastasis of the mesentery
removal of well-differentiated neuroendocrine carcinoma of the ileum
treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 15 × 10 mm polypoid tumor, en bloc resection of 80 cm ileum
treatment_information.treatment_response: Significant clinical improvement, remission of hypercortisolism and diabetes mellitus

treatment_timeline.event: Patient presented with weight gain, muscle tremor, general fatigue, perspiration, hypercholesterolemia, exophthalmos, enlargement of the thyroid gland, and cushingoid features
Subtotal thyroidectomy and removal of pituitary lesion performed
Treatment started with metyrapone combined with hydrocortisone replacement
Removal of ACTH-producing metastasis of the mesentery
Removal of well-differentiated neuroendocrine carcinoma of the ileum
Follow-up examinations at 2, 7, 9, and 18 months post-surgery

patient_outcome.final_status: Remission of hypercortisolism and diabetes mellitus
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Normal dexamethasone suppression test, no evidence of diabetes mellitus, normal bone densitometry at spine and hip
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Improved physical fitness and mood, although still suffered from obesity and hypercholesterolemia
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Felt fine, regained strength and muscle mass, did not need further potassium replacement
